programme.desc: Part one of two. The agents investigate when two men are killed at a gun shop, but soon discover one of their colleagues could be at risk. programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Chameleon's planted clues lead the team on a wild goose chase as it searches for NSA agent Mike Atley, who the criminal claims to have in his custody. programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant makes a confession to Nigel, but leaves Phil to speculate about his plans. Sharon takes a last look at the world she grew up in

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Gina has her hands full when a high-powered visitor decides to take a tour of the station - where Terry is nursing his wounds from love rival David Coles and the custody office has become a crime scene after a rowdy domestic turns into a stabbing incident. The beleaguered Inspector comes up smiling when the eligible bigwig asks her out on a date. Linda Robson and Nicola Stapleton guest star

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Chrissie decides she no longer wants to be a nurse and feels able to offer only moral rather than medical assistance to a boy injured in a car accident. Connie realises she can never break up John's family and ends her relationship with him, all the while fighting to save his son's life. Joseph hopes that bringing the wedding forward will end Faye's insecurity over his desire to be a father

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A new recruit arrives, but someone else has already turned up claiming to be the same person, leaving Tess with a mystery to solve. Comfort struggles to cope with memories of the train crash, prompting her to question her religious beliefs. Keith refuses to accept Claire's answer to his marriage proposal. Josh finds himself in hot water after making bad decisions at the scene of an accident

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Will gets involved in an affray outside a nightclub and finds his career is on the line - even though the incident gives the team enough evidence to foil a raid on a jeweller's shop. Zain's father is arrested on suspicion of burglary, but it turns out he is on a more high-minded mission on behalf of a friend. After a night of passion, Gina discovers her new conquest is a married man

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant gets everyone to go to Quiz Night. Liam tries to get Clare away from Nigel, Sharon tells Pauline about Grant, David cannot cope with his feelings for Bianca

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant makes a confession to Nigel, but leaves Phil to speculate about his plans. Sharon takes a last look at the world she grew up in

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Jack heads out around town with Sarah on her first day at work, but the horrors of the war cast a shadow over Inverness. Meanwhile, Olivia is unable to stop herself from wondering about the woman James admitted to loving in Europe, and makes a shocking discovery. Drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp, Craig Hall, Arianwen Parkes-Lockwood and David Berry

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Andrew and Kate offer moral support to an alcoholic woman fighting to keep custody of her children, and find themselves growing closer together in the process. Meanwhile, over at the Black Swan, Mike and Liz try to digest unexpected news. Gary Mavers, Melanie Kilburn, John Bowler and Lynsey Baxter star

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Flamboyant vet Granville Bennett makes an impression on James with his generosity. Rural drama, guest starring James Grout (Inspector Morse), with Christopher Timothy and Robert Hardy

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: William decides to complain to the mayor, after he and Hester are kept awake by the nocturnal activities of a local farmer. Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie star

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tests confirm Tom has prostate trouble and Harvey is excited when a representative from Bayview has been chosen to attend the Council of European Old People. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: As Michelle makes arrangements to send a microfilm to England by homing pigeon, Herr Flick captures Yvette and Mimi in order to gain access to the cafe to search for the painting of the Fallen Madonna. However, the town's annual Fishmongers' Parade proves a hindrance for all concerned. Gordon Kaye and Kirsten Cooke star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Barry buys Glenda a chaise longue from Auntie Wainwright's shop as an anniversary present, but it soon proves to be more than just something to sit on. Meanwhile, Howard finds himself locked in Pearl's bedroom with no means of escape, so Truly, Clegg and Billy set out on a rescue mission. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis and Mike Grady

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The duo are enlisted to design a memorial garden based on the myth of Orpheus and Eurydice, but someone begins vandalising their work on a nightly basis. Matters are complicated still further by the death of a recovering alcoholic, whose body is found at the site. Mystery drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris, with Phyllida Law, Joanna David and Daisy Dunlop

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Another mystery for Ardal O'Hanlon as Irish detective Jack Mooney working on the Caribbean island of Saint Marie, where the sun shines all day and it seems half the residents are killers-in-waiting. And so it appears this week as someone from JP's past is caught red-handed at a murder scene, raking up old memories for the young officer. But can the DI unravel the complex mystery to get at the truth? Tobi Bakare and Danny John-Jules co-star, with guest appearances from Ralph Brown and Adjoa Andoh

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Back home in Bermondsey for his youngest daughter's wedding, Gerry is visited by Ralph Paxton, an old friend recently released from prison who wants him investigate the murder of his grandson. Meanwhile, a newly divorced Sasha is forced to work with ex-husband Ned Hancock, Danny puts the body language skills he has acquired on an FBI course to the test, and Gerry's daughter Caitlin gets upset when her father interrogates her fiance about his credentials. Cold case drama, starring Dennis Waterman, Tamzin Outhwaite and Nicholas Lyndhurst, with a guest appearance by David Hayman (Trial & Retribution)

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: A former spy visits Thames House - but it soon becomes clear she has more on her mind than catching up with old friends. Armed with a bomb, she holds the team hostage - demanding its members produce evidence that the security services assassinated Diana, Princess of Wales. Guest starring Lindsay Duncan, with Peter Firth, Rupert Penry-Jones and Anna Chancellor

Zain's father is arrested on suspicion of burglary, but it turns out he is on a more high-minded mission on behalf of a friend. programme.desc: James saves a dog and a marriage, while Tristan receives an unexpected visit from an old flame. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.desc: Rene and the others make the final preparations for the parade, while the general, the colonel and Gruber play a round of golf, during which they discuss the plot to blow up Hitler

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Tom attempts to step into his father's shoes by riding Compo's bicycle and, enthused by Clegg and Truly's anecdotes, he sets about performing wonders of his own on the two-wheeled bone-shaker. Meanwhile, Pearl is delighted when Howard gets stuck on the roof, enabling her to keep an eye on him, and Glenda is dismayed to learn Barry has challenged a member of the office staff to a duel - refereed by Billy

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth receives a cryptic letter from a recently deceased acquaintance asking her to conduct a mysterious investigation. Instructed only to go on a coach tour of historic homes around England, she uncovers murder and intrigue. Feature-length mystery, starring Joan Hickson, with Peter Tilbury, John Horsley and Helen Cherry

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team re-examines the murder of a junior doctor strangled in 2010. Interviewing her relatives, who are all doctors, staff at her hospital and a surgeon who had been the young woman's tutor, officers hope to identify a lead that was missed during the original investigation. Depressed at the thought of his daughter Holly leaving home for university, Danny finds himself being cheered by his colleagues as they hire a pedibus for a ride through central London. Tamzin Outhwaite and Nicholas Lyndhurst star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Adam returns to work when multiple terrorist attacks plunge Britain into chaos. The team's investigation soon reveals there could be an enemy within - and suspicion falls on rival intelligence agency MI6. Rupert Penry-Jones stars

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Heavy snow disrupts plans for the wedding as travel to Button House becomes near impossible, so Alison and Mike have to rally round to make sure Sam and Clare's big day goes ahead. The Captain is in his element with the emergency preparations to the house, but Pat is distracted by an unexpected reminder of his past, while Lady Button is aghast at what Alison is allowing to take place at her former home

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Gavin & Stacey co-creator and star Ruth Jones traces her family tree, learning that her paternal grandfather was a leading figure in the Medical Aid Societies of South Wales - which provided a model for the National Health Service in 1948. On her maternal side, she discovers that her great-great-great-grandfather was a mariner who rose to the rank of captain in the domestic coastal trade

programme.title: Stand Up for Live Comedy
programme.desc: Kiri Pritchard-McLean hosts as the showcase for emerging comic talent visits Dreamland's Roller Room in Margate, introducing three guests. Vlad Ilich shares his unique take on being a millennial Eastern European living in the UK, Priya Hall reveals what it's like to grow up as a mixed-race kid in the Welsh valleys, and Jonny Pelham talks about manning up, finding love and being weird.

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: First episode of the comedy sketch show, featuring a misunderstood superhero, Croydon's leading witch doctor, a raunchy morning chat show, a grime MC who is an expert in royal history, a preview of the latest Nigerian blockbuster movie, and some much-needed cultural diversity in Midsomer Murders

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Jennifer Saunders hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Matt Forde and Ayesha Hazarika joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host offers a wry look at some trending news stories before welcoming a galaxy of stars - both in the studio and online - for a chat. Rock legend Bruce Springsteen promotes his new album Letter to You, Kristin Scott Thomas talks about the new Netflix adaptation of Rebecca and Oscar-winner Matthew McConaughey discusses his memoir Greenlights. Comedian Mawaan Rizwan promotes the new season of Taskmaster, while actor Stephen Mangan talks about Sky Arts' Portrait Artist of the Year. Plus, musical guest Sam Smith chats and performs current single Diamonds

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a two-bed semi in Bilston, a quirky end terrace with an annex in Scholar Green, Stoke-on-Trent, and a flat in Croydon

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Investigating cases of coach drivers who continue to drive while exhausted. Plus, new imaging technology used to discover if sheds are being illegally rented out as homes

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Nick Knowles presents footage of an angler fishing on a rock who is swept into a raging sea, and a mountain biker whose attempt at a stunt does not go according to plan

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams look for items at the Royal Highland Centre in Ingliston, Edinburgh, with help from experts Roo Irvine and David Harper. Natasha Raskin Sharp keeps an eye on proceedings and also finds a very special portrait

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Consumers get to grips with educational artistic toys, cleaning products designed for getting rid of pet hair, and a pregnancy extension panel for a walking jacket

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin introduces a London couple to his home town of Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire, taking them on a trip to the nearby Grand Union Canal and showcasing the local craft ales

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades enlists woodworking expert Ollie Allen to give a coffee table a makeover, while upcycler Jacqui Joseph breathes new life into a set of dining chairs

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers and valuation expert Simon Bower welcome four more people with interesting items they'd like to sell. Nigel then invites the dealers to bid against one another to secure a vintage fairground game, a 19th-century Chinese inkwell, a 1950s toy pedal car and a Victorian watchman's clock

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones brings you the stories that matter from across the country

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Money is tight for the Slaters so Kush sells Lily's tablet, but when Suki reminds him their rent is going up on Friday, he resorts to his online poker game yet again. After bumping into Honey on the way to her date, Billy tells Jay he's worried she may get hurt. Sharon tells Ian that Bobby has asked her and Max to be the foundation trustees

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming: Compilation
programme.desc: Mike and Michael are forced to call the police when a confrontation at a Yorkshire car dealership turns ugly. Can they get the man who was sold a faulty car the money he is owed? Rob and Gerald are called to remove a group of squatters from a family business. The unwelcome guests have racked up thousands of pounds in utility bills leaving the owner out of pocket and the place a mess. Tommy goes in search of a debtor on the beautiful Welsh coast and it seems a boat removal will be the most likely outcome

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: Gail develops a new outlook on life, but must also face the consequences of her actions. Belle's life is thrown into further chaos when her sister moves in, but she also ends up providing David with the answers he seeks. However, deciding what to do next will require him to question what he really believes. Hannah spends her hen night troubled by indecision, until a chat with Gail sheds light on both their problems

programme.title: Rent Like a Boss
programme.desc: Estate agents help young people find the best rental properties on tiny budgets. The first episode features 25-year-old Dan from Wigan, who is looking to move to central Manchester, and 21-year-old Ciaran who lives at home in Belfast

programme.title: Rent Like a Boss
programme.desc: Big V and Tobias travel to Newcastle to help Kahina try to exit her tenancy due to black mould even though she has no break clause in her contract. They also continue to help Dan with his flat search in Manchester

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to Wales, taking a glamorous road trip in the newest grand tourers from Bentley, Aston Martin and McLaren, before taking on their German rivals and Formula One legend Heinz-Harald Frentzen. Plus, a tribute to Stirling Moss.

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Cycling great Chris Hoy, former England defender Stuart Pearce, world champion swimmer Keri-Anne Payne and Great Britain 400m star Perri Shakes-Drayton join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: The Friday night shift takes its toll on the ambulance service crews in the capital as they deal with reports of an armed male and two stabbings. Meanwhile, Adam and Orani are dispatched to an elderly man who is seeing things, while Celia tries to get an ambulance to a patient having a nervous breakdown for over four hours

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host this weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore a large ground-floor flat in Thornton Heath, Croydon, a former restaurant in Maidstone, Kent, and a terraced house in Liverpool

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers hunt for counterfeit and dangerous goods at Suffolk's Felixstowe Port. Elsewhere, a gang who callously dumped tons of illegal waste are tracked down

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A father of five is left fighting for his life after collapsing at a trampoline park, and a paraglider gets into trouble when he loses height at an alarming rate

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams test their antiques knowledge in Brighton with the help of experts Danny Sebastian and John Cameron. Presented by Charlie Ross, who also commemorates the 75th anniversary of D-Day with a visit to the D-Day Story in Portsmouth

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers unbox, test and score a vegan skincare range, a coat for drying dogs, and a device designed to attach items of hand luggage together

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple who want to swap life in a hamlet outside Chester for an attractive Suffolk town

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades and a team of experts transform a pair of chairs, a collection of metal racks and drawers, and a school desk at Walsall Recycling Centre

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are offered to chance to bid on a vintage table hockey game, an early-20th-century glass chandelier and antique sketch books that belonged to the artist Henry Thomson

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio, presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: The Great Staycation
programme.desc: Mathew Baynton narrates a documentary following the fortunes of a luxury boutique hotel in the Essex town as owners Clare and Javed try to pull off a successful bank holiday. Fully booked, short on staff and with a heatwave looming, tensions are rising in this family-run operation. But will the visitors love it or loathe it?

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Ceramics conservator Kirsten Ramsay is charged with the task of repairing a little cat teapot with an extraordinary wartime story behind it, while Dominic Chinea takes on the restoration of a once brightly coloured wooden carousel horse. The team also brings a wooden boat puzzle and a Victorian magic lantern back to life

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Crewmates Adam and Dan treat a patient who has fallen down a flight of stairs in the casino, while Rachel and Stuart respond to a call when a child falls from a bunk bed. As the shift continues, a distressing call from a patient threatening to jump into the river takes priority, and the call handler must try to keep the patient talking until help can arrive

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: Six unsigned MCs compete to become the next rap superstar, as they go head-to-head in a battle to sign for rap legends Krept and Konan's record label. In the first episode of the new series, the artists perform for the first time in front of rap legends Krept, Konan, and DJ Target, demonstrating their A Capella skills, before rapping about a key moment in their life to guest mentor Rapman

programme.title: Rap Trip: Underground Scenes Uncovered
programme.desc: Rappers FOS and Ransom travel to Liverpool to learn about Scouse Rap. They meet a 16-year-old female drill artist and a rapper from the Wirral who raps about his autism

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams of experts compete as veterans Thomas Forrester, Philip Serrell and Kate Bliss take on more recent additions Tim Weeks, Danny Sebastian and Roo Irvine. As a team they each have one hour and £300 to buys three items to take to auction, but to turn up the heat they will also be shopping on their own in a relay. Presented by Natasha Raskin Sharp

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades saves a drop-leaf table, an old wingback chair and a collection of metal baskets from the tip, hoping he and two others can make money by restoring the items

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Two siblings seek dietary advice for their parents, who have let healthy eating fall by the wayside after a stressful year dealing with bereavement and caring for an elderly mother. Gregg Wallace demonstrates that a satisfying meal can be made easily from fresh ingredients without their frequently used cooking sauces, while Chris Bavin suggests healthy homemade alternatives to takeaways

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Westminster, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold presents highlights of the latest fixtures, including St Helens v Leeds Rhinos, Wigan Warriors v Salford Red Devils and Catalans Dragons v Hull FC

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Philip Serrell, Melissa Downhill and Micaela Sharp head to Hay-on-Wye in Powys, where they have a day to find bargains they can turn into a profit

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Now they are officially an item, Fiona and George decide to host a dinner party for the whole of Weld, but George doesn't have a big enough table. In fact, they soon realise they may have bitten off a little more than they can chew and this might have come a bit early for both of them

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The contestants are asked to insert an invisible zip and pockets on a pair of palazzo pants, while the judges keep a keen eye on how well they pattern-match the bold prints. For the transformation challenge, the sewers must turn towels into stylish beach cover-ups in just 90 minutes. Male models arrive in the Sewing Room for the final challenge, ready to be fitted with a made-to-measure holiday shirt using bright and colourful fabrics. Whose shirt will bring on the sunshine, and who will be sent packing?

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Martyn Colbeck and Cynthia Moss examine the legacy of Echo the elephant, who died in 2009 after being followed on film during the final chapter of her life, having guided her family for half a century. With a drought devastating the animals' home in the shadow of Kilimanjaro, a 38-strong band of relatives and descendants must overcome the loss of their leader, hunger and the threat of poachers to survive. Presented by David Attenborough

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Presenter Paul Martin and experts Natasha Raskin Sharp and James Lewis visit Bristol Cathedral, where Natasha is intrigued by an unusual walking stick with a top made from a German Zeppelin, and James comes across a collection of lead soldiers. Paul learns about the origins of Bristol's vibrant street art scene and has a go at making a graffiti stencil.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jayde Adams, Josie d'Arby, Rufus Hound and Scott Mills go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball and Rylan Clark-Neal return with the fanzine devoted to the ballroom dancing show, looking at all the news and gossip from the first weekend of the competition

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Mark chapman presents coverage of the draw, as the non-league clubs to have progressed to the first-round and the teams from League One and Two await their fate

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair to answer questions on the specialist subjects of the Montgomery bus boycott, The Crown, the history of anaesthesia, and Stormzy, before each is tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another first-round match as Dungeons and Dragons players the Dungeon Masters take on Welsh border team the Severns. They must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Bottersnikes, Wayne Szalinski's kids, Mrs Pepperpot, and Ant-Man

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions in the second and final of the highest-scoring loser matches, as two sets of students get a last opportunity to earn a place in the next stage of the competition

programme.title: Inside the Batcave
programme.desc: Lucy Cooke presents a documentary taking a journey into the secret world of one of the most endangered and least understood animals on Earth - bats. With cutting-edge night-vision cameras and ultrasonic detectors, this programme follows a greater horseshoe bat roost for four months, capturing the hidden life of the colony as never before, witnessing the birth of a new generation of pups and tracking their progress towards their perilous maiden flights outside the roost

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts an isolation version of his topical comedy show from his garage. A diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life - including Romesh's mother - are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today and the latest talking points in the media. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined remotely by a selection of celebrity guests, comedians and experts, to investigate how and why the events in the news matter to people across the country

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: The Trump Show
programme.desc: Key players including Rudy Giuliani, Stormy Daniels, Steve Bannon and Ambassador John Bolton shed light on Trump's second year, as he faces challenges from his past

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Joe Crowley visits Box Hill in Surrey to take on its zigzag cycle route - pitting himself against double Olympic gold medal-winning cyclist Joanna Rowsell Shand. Plus, there are clips from the Countryfile archives showcasing the best thrills and spills in the countryside

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: A call comes in from London Fire Brigade requesting assistance at a fire in a block of flats which immediately puts the team under pressure. Meanwhile, mother and daughter team Nicola and Maisie have just finished with their first patient of the night but are immediately dispatched to a 12-year-old boy who has had an unfortunate accident

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Chris Beardshaw and wife Frances take on a new allotment and are starting from scratch to create an easy, low-maintenance fruit and vegetable patch. Kirsty Wilson is looking to extend the season by using late-flowering perennials that complement autumn colours and Brian Cunningham takes advantage of the season to plant cost-effective bare root trees. To conclude the season, the team enjoys a video call - including a surprise visitor to the party

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A former policeman in Northern Ireland forced to rebuild his life after a rocket attack on his vehicle and a Leeds paramedic threatened by a hospital patient with a 12-inch knife

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Officers raid a Birmingham sweatshop suspected of being a major supplier of counterfeit clothing, and pursue a builder who rendered a family home unfit to be lived in

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore unearths three hidden gems that she believes have profit-making potential. She calls on lighting expert Guy Trench to find a new use for a collection of auction cast-offs, while designer Daniel Heath has a tallboy chest of drawers to get creative with. A flatpack dining set is given the upcycling treatment by Sarah herself

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don prepares his pond for the season ahead and prunes a climbing rose. Adam Frost offers ideas for a winter display of containers and the programme catches up with Frances Tophill on her allotment in Kent, to find out about her growing year. Plus, another chance to see Carol Klein's autumnal visit to Hidcote Gardens in Gloucestershire, and a couple whose garden has been inspired by the Far East

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: A team of three must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Fine art dealer Gavin Claxton, trader Linda Lambert and master upholsterer Anthony Devine head to Oxford to search for bargains

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Maxine Peake presents an appeal on behalf of Jessie's Fund, a UK charity helping disabled and seriously ill children through the use of music

programme.title: Live Women's International Football
programme.desc: Germany v England (Kick-off 3.00pm). All the action from the friendly at Brita Arena in Wiesbaden. The Germans prevailed 2-1 when the teams last met back in November, and Phil Neville's face a tough task as they look to avenge that defeat having lost 21 of the previous 26 meetings against a side currently ranked second in the world behind America

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin hosts from Rochester Cathedral in Kent, where antiques expert Elizabeth Talbot falls for a book of fairy tales illustrated by Arthur Rackham, and Adam Partridge discovers an elegant chiming Swiss pocket watch. Paul also meets one of the last remaining survivors of the Dunkirk evacuation during the Second World War

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jayde Adams, Josie d'Arby, Rufus Hound and Scott Mills go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball takes a closer look at the weekend's performances in Choreography Corner, and more couples join her on the sofa for a chat

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Diversity dancers Jordan Banjo and Perri Kiely join experts James Braxton and Phil Serrell on the hunt for bargains to sell at auction. The celebrity pair discover an ancient Chinese leisure activity on a river in Cambridgeshire, before heading to their final destination at an auction in Greenwich

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan and Gillian Burke return to track the changes in nature throughout autumn

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: A 75-year-old patient has an enlarged aorta, the main vessel for carrying oxygenated blood to the body. Her condition could cause her to die at any time - but treating it also poses a risk to her life. At Addenbrooke's hospital, a patient with a genetic condition that makes her susceptible to cancer undergoes treatment to remove a polyp that has the potential to turn into cancer

programme.title: Out of Her Mind
programme.desc: Sara decides to face up to her sadness about the past and bravely attempts to return her unused but tattered wedding dress to the shop where Craig jilted her 15 years ago. Sitcom, starring Sara Pascoe

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: After entering the local line dancing endurance challenge Mandy finds herself up against an old rival. Comedy, starring Diane Morgan, with Maxine Peake

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Enslaved with Samuel L Jackson
programme.desc: A look at the campaign to bring the slave trade to an end after 400 years. Divers explore the sunken remains of a ship that had been carrying freed slaves who had been recaptured. Having traced his own ancestry back to the Benga people of Gabon, Samuel visits the country to learn more of their traditions and stories. Plus, a trip to Costa Rica in search of evidence of escapees from two Danish slave ships

programme.title: The Grand Party Hotel
programme.desc: A busy Friday sees almost 700 party-mad people checking in. But with just minutes to go, there are problems with the plumbing. It's even worse for Charley, who has arrived for a hen party organised by her brother, as there are problems with their room - and general manager Damian struggles to placate them. Meanwhile, owners Laurence and Katie throw an extravagant circus-themed party for the twins' fourth birthday - and all the staff are invited

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: The comedian looks back at the art of making a historical documentary. He examines a wide range of shows, including Kenneth Clarke's Civilisation, the works of AJP Taylor, James Burke and Mary Beard, all the way to Who Do You Think You Are and Danny Dyer's Right Royal Family, to discover what it takes to make a great TV history lesson

programme.title: The Trump Show
programme.desc: Four years after Donald Trump unexpectedly became president of the most powerful nation on earth, this documentary series tells the inside story of this most extraordinary presidency, with contributions from key players including Steve Bannon, Sean Spicer, Omarosa Manigault Newman and Anthony Scaramucci. The first episode charts the first 18 months, covering Trump's change of lifestyle as he moved into the White House, his fraught relationship with the press and his downgrading of White House briefings - preferring instead to talk directly to the public through Twitter

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A spate of burglaries at Christmas leads to a police community support officer and his colleague being attacked, as they track down and try to arrest the thief. A West Midlands police officer returns to work after an assault, only to be attacked again just weeks later. Plus, a West Yorkshire police officer starts his retirement needing physiotherapy after being attacked

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Investigating cases of coach drivers who continue to drive while exhausted. Plus, new imaging technology used to discover if sheds are being illegally rented out as homes

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades enlists woodworking expert Ollie Allen to give a coffee table a makeover, while upcycler Jacqui Joseph breathes new life into a set of dining chairs

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including St Helens v Salford Red Devils, Leeds Rhinos v Castleford Tigers and Wakefield Trinity v Hull Kingston Rovers

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: The genealogy documentary follows more celebrities as they trace their family trees to uncover facts about their heritage. First up is Doctor Who star Jodie Whittaker, who looks into a romantic story she has been told about how her paternal grandmother Greta came to be given the middle name of Verdun - which was a First World War battle. On her mother's side, Jodie gets to the bottom of how her great-great-grandfather worked his way up from child labourer in a Yorkshire coal mine to becoming the owner of one

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: Quiz show in which a team of three must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Philip Serrell, Tony Wong and Micaela Sharp search Stratford-upon-Avon for items, choosing a bottle-corking machine, some lamps and old furniture in the hope of turning a profit

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Arlo is desperate to go into catering and gives up his major school subjects, much to the concern of George. Woody thinks Tracey is taking on too much with her new job

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The ten remaining home sewers return to the nation's most famous sewing room for children's week, and are asked to make a classic smocked dress in the pattern challenge. The task requires the sewers to combine tricky techniques, including making their own bias binding, and a Peter Pan collar. Next, in the transformation challenge, the sewers let their imaginations run wild, turning sleeping bags into food shaped fancy dress. Finally, the contestants are asked to make a pair of dungarees for a little boy or girl

programme.title: Italy's Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Using the latest 3D scanning technology, Alexander Armstrong and Dr Michael Scott uncover the hidden history of three Italian cities, beginning with Naples, from its earliest Greek and Roman origins. The pair explore the impact of the volcano of Mount Vesuvius, and how it both nurtured the region and exacted a terrible price on the local population. Alexander and Michael also delve into a labyrinth of underground spaces constructed by ruling Bourbon kings in the 19th century, that helped to sustain the city

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A special episode revisiting some of the historic venues visited during this series, including Bamburgh Castle in Northumberland

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jayde Adams, Josie d'Arby, Rufus Hound and Scott Mills go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball gives all the backstage gossip and exclusive news from Strictly Come Dancing, and Ian Waite gives his thoughts on the couples' training progress.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Peter Andre and Joe Pasquale travel around Kent in search of antiques, assisted by experts Paul Laidlaw and Catherine Southon. Joe persuades expert Catherine Southon to buy a pommel horse, while Paul Laidlaw impresses his celebrity sidekick Peter with a pressed poppy from the battlefields of the First World War

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams all look at the unseen side of autumn from their four separate locations across the country. Tonight, Michaela shares more drama from the Scottish seal colony, and Chris reveals the latest antics of the multi-coloured badger family

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Charlotte is determined to get her own back on Isaac Pincher who has burned down her house. Lucy suggests a novel way of drumming up business for her new enterprise with Elizabeth and Fredo Harvey. Meanwhile, Lydia makes a new friend in Bedlam

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits the historic city of Cadiz in southern Spain, where he enjoys traditional tapas dishes including chickpea stews, cured pork lardons and freshly grilled mackerel

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: A light-hearted look at the social and cultural changes society went through since the turn of the 21st century, featuring contributions from politicians and celebrities. In the second episode, Angela Scanlon, Amol Rajan and Emma Barnett take a light-hearted look back at the year 2001, from the first Harry Potter film to hit cinemas, to a gripping EastEnders storyline

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: The Dark Shadow of Murder
programme.desc: The mother of murder victim Moira Jones shares her personal account of learning to cope with grief and using her experience to help others coping with similar tragedies

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The Fraserburgh crew race to save a stricken fishing boat without a working rudder in a force-eight gale. On Loch Ness, a yacht becomes trapped on a weir, and one wrong move by the lifeboat crew could send it over the edge. On the north Devon coast, the Minehead crew comes to the aid of a puppy that has swallowed a fishing hook

programme.title: The Shipman Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: The final edition opens by looking at the first report of the public inquiry into the deaths of Harold Shipman's patients, concluding that while working as a GP he had murdered 260 of them. Chris Wilson explores the reaction to the staggering total, exploring how attention began to focus on the killer as experts and commentators came up with a range of psychological profiles and theories to try to understand why one, seemingly ordinary doctor had killed so many people

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness each buy a sporty insurance write-off for £6,000 and take part in three challenges at the programme's test track before they're ordered to make their cars road legal and report to Alexandra Palace, where the producers have constructed a monstrous Wall of Death. The trio also celebrate the era of the 200mph supercar by going on a road trip in a Ferrari F40, a Lamborghini Diablo, and a Jaguar XJ220

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Industrial Age
programme.desc: Fred visits the North East, a region rich in transport history, to explore the development of Britain's railways, from the first steam locomotives to the record-breaking Mallard. A visit to York's National Railway Museum offers the chance to ride a replica of Stephenson's Rocket, while at Ffestiniog Railway he learns about the computer-aided design process used for new locomotives

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene featuring a cabin in a winter wonderland, complete with soft hills bedecked in purple

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and art expert Philip Mould visit a B&B in Aberdeenshire to pick up where the owner's late husband left off in proving that a painting is the work of 19th-century French artist Paul Delaroche. A visit to the British museum raises evidence that this could be a lost treasure once belonging to France's last king and queen, and they head for Normandy to inspect a stained glass window in the image of a lost Delaroche painting - but they also begin to suspect it is a copy, rather than the original

programme.title: Michael Palin's Quest for Artemisia
programme.desc: The Monty Python star sets off on a quest to investigate the story of little-known 17th-century Italian artist Artemisia Gentileschi after one of her paintings caught his eye. Michael encounters her work in Florence, Rome and Naples, and finds out about her life as a working mother and her ultimate success against all odds as a great painter of the Baroque age who transformed the way women were depicted in art, and who was sought after in many courts across Europe

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of how a cute cartoon character became an international hate symbol after being hijacked by the alt-right, following Pepe's creator Matt Furie as he fights to bring back his lovable comic-book character from the dark forces who stole him

programme.title: How to Go Viral: The Art of the Meme with Richard Clay
programme.desc: Art historian Professor Richard Clay explores how internet memes are shaping culture and politics, and experiments by devising and releasing some himself

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and art expert Philip Mould visit a B&B in Aberdeenshire to pick up where the owner's late husband left off in proving that a painting is the work of 19th-century French artist Paul Delaroche. A visit to the British museum raises evidence that this could be a lost treasure once belonging to France's last king and queen, and they head for Normandy to inspect a stained glass window in the image of a lost Delaroche painting - but they also begin to suspect it is a copy, rather than the original

programme.title: Michael Palin's Quest for Artemisia

Michael encounters her work in Florence, Rome and Naples, and finds out about her life as a working mother and her ultimate success against all odds as a great painter of the Baroque age who transformed the way women were depicted in art, and who was sought after in many courts across Europe

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Industrial Age
programme.desc: The former steeplejack recalls the golden age of shipbuilding, examining the skills of engineers who turned Britain into one of the leading manufacturing nations, and visits Bristol to pay tribute to his personal hero Isambard Kingdom Brunel. He also travels to Scotland for a voyage on the paddle steamer Waverley

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross's dramatic use of blue tones results in an emotional depiction of crashing night-time waves - all tucked inside an oval

programme.title: Vikings
programme.desc: Neil Oliver continues his attempt to shed new light on the Norse warriors by examining their sophisticated trading skills, and visits locations in Russia, Turkey and Ireland to explain how they established a business empire. He also explores the vast array of goods that were bought and sold by Viking merchants and settlers, including Chinese silks and Pictish slaves

programme.title: Blood of the Clans
programme.desc: Neil Oliver uncovers the story of Rob Roy MacGregor, who has for centuries been celebrated as a Highland maverick, as a well-intentioned rogue - Scotland's answer to Robin Hood

programme.title: Play for Today
programme.desc: Just a Boys' Game by Peter McDougall. A young man who grew up idolising his grandfather for his apparent toughness sees another side to the old man as he nears the end of his life - and discovers his own value in the process. Starring Frankie Miller and Ken Hutchison

programme.title: Spinning a Yarn: The Dubious History of Scottish Tartan
programme.desc: Documentary looking at how the patterned fabric has been inextricably woven into the history of Scotland, from Bonnie Prince Charlie to the Tartan Army, and used to advertise many political and cultural affinities. Moray Hunter narrates the story of its murky past and colourful present, taking in the Englishmen who forged a guide to clan tartans, Walter Scott's pageant of 1822 and the 21st-century Scottish Register of Tartans

programme.title: DNA
programme.desc: Rolf Larsen, a respected detective on the Copenhagen police force, investigates the case of a missing child, but his life is brutally upended when his own baby daughter goes missing. Five years after the event, a new lead emerges when a serious flaw is uncovered in the police's DNA database. In Danish, English, Polish and French

programme.title: DNA
programme.desc: A stolen car in Thornby results in Rolf meeting young police assistant Neel and the detective is reluctantly ordered by their joint boss to test the car thief's DNA with his old colleagues in Copenhagen. In Danish, English, Polish and French

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross's dramatic use of blue tones results in an emotional depiction of crashing night-time waves - all tucked inside an oval

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Industrial Age

programme.title: Blood of the Clans
programme.desc: Neil Oliver uncovers the story of Rob Roy MacGregor, who has for centuries been celebrated as a Highland maverick, as a well-intentioned rogue - Scotland's answer to Robin Hood

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Magnificent Monuments
programme.desc: The former steeplejack examines the development of castles from early Iron Age forts to secret tunnels used during the Second World War. He visits Hadrian's Wall, where he takes a look at the remains of Roman toilets, while in Warwick a band of medieval knights tests the castle's defences

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and advice as demonstrates his painting technique, beginning with a picture of mountains, evergreens, paths and bushes

programme.title: The Fairytale Castles of King Ludwig II with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the aesthetic styles associated with Ludwig II of Bavaria, the king who was considered to be handsome and loved by his people. Dan Cruickshank investigates the mock-medievalism of Neuschwanstein castle and the complex design of Herrenchiemsee, to assess how they are key to learning more about the enigmatic ruler

programme.title: The Fall of the Berlin Wall with John Simpson
programme.desc: To mark the 30th anniversary of the collapse of the Berlin Wall on November 9, 1989, John Simpson, the BBC's longest-serving correspondent, goes back to his reports on what he believes to be the most important story he has ever covered. Back then, John thought the even would change the world for the better, for ever. Three decades on, he looks at how history has actually turned out and whether he was wrong to have been so optimistic

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Berlin Wall
programme.desc: Originally shown to mark 20 years since the structure that divided the city was brought down, this documentary hears from the people most closely involved with the horrors it created. Spies, informers, double-agents and interrogators explain their roles in the corruption and brutalisation of the new socialist world the Wall's designers had thought they were forging

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Zionne, Isaiah and Mandi are young black teenagers who have all experienced racism growing up in the UK. Mandi has been called racist names, Isaiah is often followed round shops by security guards, and Zionne feels she will always have to work harder than everyone else to succeed. In the first of a two-part special, they speak out about what life is really like growing up black in the UK and why things urgently need to change. After the death of George Floyd and the nationwide protests against racism, the teenagers know that this is their time to be heard. As well as opening up about their own experiences, they speak to their friends and their parents. They decide to create a manifesto to list the big things they feel need to change in society to make life better for black kids like them.

programme.desc: Mum and Melody are on an outing to a Butterfly Park. Mum takes her into the Butterfly house and tells her all about the life cycle of a butterfly. Melody asks for some 'butterfly' music. Mum gives her Rhaspody in Blue by George Gershwin. As Melody settles down to listen to the music she is transported into a lovely animation all about the little blue butterfly

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee would love a garden of his own that looks as amazing as Mr Og's. Mo does her best to help him create one, but gardens take time to grow

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Fun and games with the letter L, introducing the words log, lid, bed, ted, hug, cot, nap and lips

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘top' and we go on a whirlwind word adventure.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Three does magic tricks with apples to show the others who goes first, who is biggest and how to surprise number friends

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The bunny is shocked to find there is no water in the paddling pool he visits with Sula, but soon realises there are all sorts of different games they can play instead

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Messy is having fun blowing loud notes through his musical horn instrument, but whatever noise Messy is making someone or something is making it too

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Educational programme for children aged between four and seven

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dispy, Laa-Laa and Po enjoy songtime, singing solo and then singing together

programme.title: Tinpo
programme.desc: Leaning Tower of Tinpotown. A building doesn't want to stand up straight and the Po can't live with slanting floors

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: With Ferne Corrigan and Rory Crawford

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Finlay and his father go on a bike ride to a nearby loch. They find insects and seaweed, and Dad shows Finlay how to skim stones across the water. Narrated by Rebecca Keatley

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Captain Captain tells Cook and Line to find a new use for the ship's anchor, while anothe team of youngsters helps Gem to reclaim her jewels

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: When the Squirrels find Duggee playing saxophone, they want to have a go too

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Cotton-tail's birthday cake is stolen, but when Peter tries to get it back, he runs into a host of angry villains

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster is excited to start a Challenge Yourself Day adventure with his friends. He’s planned a mountain hike with a special sunset surprise for them at the peak. At the base of the mountain, Love Monster hands out equipment to Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny, Bad Idea Puppy and Book Cub. But when he tries to talk through his plan and warm-up, his friends play with their equipment and have fun doing their own exercises. A little frustrated, Love Monster blows his whistle to get their attention and says it’s time to start climbing.

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: In this episode, Grace shows off three amazing machines that help with farming! Grace drives a massive compost turner, shows off a machine that makes hay bales and then picks some tasty carrots in a carrot harvester!

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day travels back 220 million years to the era of the postosuchus - a giant creature that roamed North America in the time of the earliest dinosaurs

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: CGI action comedy for pre-school children following eight-year-old Bitz and her younger brother Bob as they playfully explore their love of science

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are in trouble when Glitch's feather collecting mission goes wrong.

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: Sam launches an investigation to find Laila's dad's lost phone

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Gramps teaches Waffle to read while Mrs Hobbs is dog-sitting Jelly next door

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo has a check-up at the dentist and is hoping to earn a special sticker for looking after her teeth and keeping them clean and fresh!

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The local park is closed so Sarah and Duck search for a suitable alternative

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Revival of the classic animation about the adventures of a clan of friendly aliens living on a small blue planet. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The colourful characters have fun in the garden, but their trousers keep falling down

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Rosamund Pike reads Never Ask a Dinosaur to Dinner, by Gareth Edwards and Guy Parker-Rees

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Property relocation advice programme, beginning as Steve Brown helps a couple feeling the pinch of the London property market to decide on their ideal town and the right home in which to raise their young children by showing them around Knaresborough, North Yorkshire

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore unearths three hidden gems that she believes have profit-making potential. She calls on lighting expert Guy Trench to find a new use for a collection of auction cast-offs, while designer Daniel Heath has a tallboy chest of drawers to get creative with. A flatpack dining set is given the upcycling treatment by Sarah herself

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Sellers Lindsay, Alistair, Clare and Lucinda arrive hoping to persuade dealers Ian, James, Lucy, Moses and Tash to bid against each other for a royal coin collection, two unique mirrors, a Russian-themed barometer and an ice shaver. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: The case of a 19-year-old who was scammed out of his student loan. Plus, a look at how pet scams and thefts have soared during the pandemic, and advice on protecting against fraud

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jack seeks parenting advice from a grief-stricken Mitch, who tells him to make sure he puts the kids first. Max offers Linda work at the restaurant, but when she asks Mick for his thoughts, he seems indifferent - angering Tina with his irritable behaviour. Sonia is ecstatic to receive a car as a gift from one of her recent patients, while Bobby chooses his trustees for the Lucy Beale Foundation

programme.title: Black and Welsh
programme.desc: Cardiff-born film-maker Liana Rose Stewart brings together people from across Wales to share their experiences of what it means to be black and Welsh, hearing from actors, comedians, leading business figures, influencers and heroes of their communities

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Gavin & Stacey co-creator and star Ruth Jones traces her family tree, learning that her paternal grandfather was a leading figure in the Medical Aid Societies of South Wales - which provided a model for the National Health Service in 1948. On her maternal side, she discovers that her great-great-great-grandfather was a mariner who rose to the rank of captain in the domestic coastal trade

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Coronavirus has hit the whole nation hard, but for the under-25s the impact has been particularly difficult. Reporter Kash Jones investigates the long-term consequences of the pandemic on young people, revealing exclusive research about the impact of Covid-19 on them and the scale of the challenges ahead. He meets young adults trying to deal with the impact on their education, job prospects and mental health

programme.title: Stand Up for Live Comedy
programme.desc: Kiri Pritchard-McLean hosts as the showcase for emerging comic talent visits Dreamland's Roller Room in Margate, introducing three guests. Vlad Ilich shares his unique take on being a millennial Eastern European living in the UK, Priya Hall reveals what it's like to grow up as a mixed-race kid in the Welsh valleys, and Jonny Pelham talks about manning up, finding love and being weird.

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: First episode of the comedy sketch show, featuring a misunderstood superhero, Croydon's leading witch doctor, a raunchy morning chat show, a grime MC who is an expert in royal history, a preview of the latest Nigerian blockbuster movie, and some much-needed cultural diversity in Midsomer Murders

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Jennifer Saunders hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Matt Forde and Ayesha Hazarika joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host offers a wry look at some trending news stories before welcoming a galaxy of stars - both in the studio and online - for a chat. Rock legend Bruce Springsteen promotes his new album Letter to You, Kristin Scott Thomas talks about the new Netflix adaptation of Rebecca and Oscar-winner Matthew McConaughey discusses his memoir Greenlights. Comedian Mawaan Rizwan promotes the new season of Taskmaster, while actor Stephen Mangan talks about Sky Arts' Portrait Artist of the Year. Plus, musical guest Sam Smith chats and performs current single Diamonds

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin introduces a London couple to his home town of Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire, taking them on a trip to the nearby Grand Union Canal and showcasing the local craft ales

programme.desc: Jay Blades enlists woodworking expert Ollie Allen to give a coffee table a makeover, while upcycler Jacqui Joseph breathes new life into a set of dining chairs

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones brings you the stories that matter from across the country

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Money is tight for the Slaters so Kush sells Lily's tablet, but when Suki reminds him their rent is going up on Friday, he resorts to his online poker game yet again. After bumping into Honey on the way to her date, Billy tells Jay he's worried she may get hurt. Sharon tells Ian that Bobby has asked her and Max to be the foundation trustees

programme.title: The Wedding Dress Shop
programme.desc: A bride undergoing breast cancer treatment goes for a fitting, but is shocked to discover Samm intends to take a pair of scissors to the dress while she is wearing it. A woman with fragile confidence who never normally wears dresses gives her wedding gown the seal of approval - with some support from her mother and mother-in-law

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by actors Kevin Bishop and Brian Blessed, comedian Josh Widdicombe and historian Kate Williams, who aim to help the team captains deceive their opponents with plausible lies. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: Gail develops a new outlook on life, but must also face the consequences of her actions. Belle's life is thrown into further chaos when her sister moves in, but she also ends up providing David with the answers he seeks. However, deciding what to do next will require him to question what he really believes. Hannah spends her hen night troubled by indecision, until a chat with Gail sheds light on both their problems

programme.title: Rent Like a Boss
programme.desc: Estate agents help young people find the best rental properties on tiny budgets. The first episode features 25-year-old Dan from Wigan, who is looking to move to central Manchester, and 21-year-old Ciaran who lives at home in Belfast

programme.title: Rent Like a Boss
programme.desc: Big V and Tobias travel to Newcastle to help Kahina try to exit her tenancy due to black mould even though she has no break clause in her contract. They also continue to help Dan with his flat search in Manchester

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to Wales, taking a glamorous road trip in the newest grand tourers from Bentley, Aston Martin and McLaren, before taking on their German rivals and Formula One legend Heinz-Harald Frentzen. Plus, a tribute to Stirling Moss.

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Cycling great Chris Hoy, former England defender Stuart Pearce, world champion swimmer Keri-Anne Payne and Great Britain 400m star Perri Shakes-Drayton join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: The Friday night shift takes its toll on the ambulance service crews in the capital as they deal with reports of an armed male and two stabbings. Meanwhile, Adam and Orani are dispatched to an elderly man who is seeing things, while Celia tries to get an ambulance to a patient having a nervous breakdown for over four hours

programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host this weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore a large ground-floor flat in Thornton Heath, Croydon, a former restaurant in Maidstone, Kent, and a terraced house in Liverpool

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers hunt for counterfeit and dangerous goods at Suffolk's Felixstowe Port. Elsewhere, a gang who callously dumped tons of illegal waste are tracked down

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple who want to swap life in a hamlet outside Chester for an attractive Suffolk town

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades and a team of experts transform a pair of chairs, a collection of metal racks and drawers, and a school desk at Walsall Recycling Centre

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are offered to chance to bid on a vintage table hockey game, an early-20th-century glass chandelier and antique sketch books that belonged to the artist Henry Thomson

programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio, presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Heavy snow disrupts plans for the wedding as travel to Button House becomes near impossible, so Alison and Mike have to rally round to make sure Sam and Clare's big day goes ahead. The Captain is in his element with the emergency preparations to the house, but Pat is distracted by an unexpected reminder of his past, while Lady Button is aghast at what Alison is allowing to take place at her former home

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Ceramics conservator Kirsten Ramsay is charged with the task of repairing a little cat teapot with an extraordinary wartime story behind it, while Dominic Chinea takes on the restoration of a once brightly coloured wooden carousel horse. The team also brings a wooden boat puzzle and a Victorian magic lantern back to life

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Crewmates Adam and Dan treat a patient who has fallen down a flight of stairs in the casino, while Rachel and Stuart respond to a call when a child falls from a bunk bed. As the shift continues, a distressing call from a patient threatening to jump into the river takes priority, and the call handler must try to keep the patient talking until help can arrive

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: Six unsigned MCs compete to become the next rap superstar, as they go head-to-head in a battle to sign for rap legends Krept and Konan's record label. In the first episode of the new series, the artists perform for the first time in front of rap legends Krept, Konan, and DJ Target, demonstrating their A Capella skills, before rapping about a key moment in their life to guest mentor Rapman

programme.title: Rap Trip: Underground Scenes Uncovered
programme.desc: Rappers FOS and Ransom travel to Liverpool to learn about Scouse Rap. They meet a 16-year-old female drill artist and a rapper from the Wirral who raps about his autism

programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to Wales, taking a glamorous road trip in the newest grand tourers from Bentley, Aston Martin and McLaren, before taking on their German rivals and Formula One legend Heinz-Harald Frentzen. Plus, a tribute to Stirling Moss.

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Cycling great Chris Hoy, former England defender Stuart Pearce, world champion swimmer Keri-Anne Payne and Great Britain 400m star Perri Shakes-Drayton join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: The Friday night shift takes its toll on the ambulance service crews in the capital as they deal with reports of an armed male and two stabbings. Meanwhile, Adam and Orani are dispatched to an elderly man who is seeing things, while Celia tries to get an ambulance to a patient having a nervous breakdown for over four hours

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Crewmates Adam and Dan treat a patient who has fallen down a flight of stairs in the casino, while Rachel and Stuart respond to a call when a child falls from a bunk bed. programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: Six unsigned MCs compete to become the next rap superstar, as they go head-to-head in a battle to sign for rap legends Krept and Konan's record label. programme.title: Rap Trip: Underground Scenes Uncovered
programme.desc: Rappers FOS and Ransom travel to Liverpool to learn about Scouse Rap. They meet a 16-year-old female drill artist and a rapper from the Wirral who raps about his autism

programme.title: Debate Night
programme.desc: Topical debate on the big issues affecting Scotland and beyond. Members of the public have an hour to put their questions to a panel of politicians and other public figures about the biggest stories and the things they care about the most

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host a weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical content and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Bilston, Wolverhampton, Bignall End in Staffordshire and Brimrod in Rochdale

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Officers raid a Birmingham sweatshop suspected of being a major supplier of counterfeit clothing, and pursue a builder who rendered a family home unfit to be lived in

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Featuring the rescue of a hiker who was searching for his dog when a cliff crumbled beneath his feet, while passers-by save the life of a woman who collapsed in the street

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Richard Madley and Stephanie Connell help the teams look for items at the Brackley Antiques Cellar in Northamptonshire, while Eric Knowles visits the Silverstone Experience

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander is joined by three entrepreneurs to watch customers test and score a dry shampoo, a measuring tool and a clamp for wheelie-bin lids

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Property relocation advice programme, beginning as Steve Brown helps a couple feeling the pinch of the London property market to decide on their ideal town and the right home in which to raise their young children by showing them around Knaresborough, North Yorkshire

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore unearths three hidden gems that she believes have profit-making potential. She calls on lighting expert Guy Trench to find a new use for a collection of auction cast-offs, while designer Daniel Heath has a tallboy chest of drawers to get creative with. A flatpack dining set is given the upcycling treatment by Sarah herself

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Sellers Lindsay, Alistair, Clare and Lucinda arrive hoping to persuade dealers Ian, James, Lucy, Moses and Tash to bid against each other for a royal coin collection, two unique mirrors, a Russian-themed barometer and an ice shaver. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Tricked-Out Tractors
programme.desc: Tylor Cartin, Stephen Lennon and Darren Bailey customise tractors, tackling mechanical problems and performing major makeovers. In the first edition, Irish tractor-pulling champ Darren Bailey brings in his monster machine to get it into shape, nedding it to be bigger and better to keep his crown

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jack seeks parenting advice from a grief-stricken Mitch, who tells him to make sure he puts the kids first. Max offers Linda work at the restaurant, but when she asks Mick for his thoughts, he seems indifferent - angering Tina with his irritable behaviour. Sonia is ecstatic to receive a car as a gift from one of her recent patients, while Bobby chooses his trustees for the Lucy Beale Foundation

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Coronavirus has hit the whole nation hard, but for the under-25s the impact has been particularly difficult. Reporter Kash Jones investigates the long-term consequences of the pandemic on young people, revealing exclusive research about the impact of Covid-19 on them and the scale of the challenges ahead. He meets young adults trying to deal with the impact on their education, job prospects and mental health

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Gavin & Stacey co-creator and star Ruth Jones traces her family tree, learning that her paternal grandfather was a leading figure in the Medical Aid Societies of South Wales - which provided a model for the National Health Service in 1948. On her maternal side, she discovers that her great-great-great-grandfather was a mariner who rose to the rank of captain in the domestic coastal trade

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Stephen Walker meets some of Northern Ireland's oldest citizens as he talks to four women and one man born in 1921, the same year that the state came into existence

programme.title: Stand Up for Live Comedy
programme.desc: Kiri Pritchard-McLean hosts as the showcase for emerging comic talent visits Dreamland's Roller Room in Margate, introducing three guests. Vlad Ilich shares his unique take on being a millennial Eastern European living in the UK, Priya Hall reveals what it's like to grow up as a mixed-race kid in the Welsh valleys, and Jonny Pelham talks about manning up, finding love and being weird.

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: First episode of the comedy sketch show, featuring a misunderstood superhero, Croydon's leading witch doctor, a raunchy morning chat show, a grime MC who is an expert in royal history, a preview of the latest Nigerian blockbuster movie, and some much-needed cultural diversity in Midsomer Murders

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Jennifer Saunders hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Matt Forde and Ayesha Hazarika joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host offers a wry look at some trending news stories before welcoming a galaxy of stars - both in the studio and online - for a chat. Rock legend Bruce Springsteen promotes his new album Letter to You, Kristin Scott Thomas talks about the new Netflix adaptation of Rebecca and Oscar-winner Matthew McConaughey discusses his memoir Greenlights. Comedian Mawaan Rizwan promotes the new season of Taskmaster, while actor Stephen Mangan talks about Sky Arts' Portrait Artist of the Year. Plus, musical guest Sam Smith chats and performs current single Diamonds

programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather
